description: First RFC 3161 timestamp token (.tsr) has one byte (offset 200 or last byte if shorter) XOR'd with 0x55. PKCS#7 SignedData structure remains parseable but signature verification of that single token fails.

modifications.mutation: Byte at offset 200 (or length-1, whichever smaller) XOR'd with 0x55.
modifications.rationale: Documents V1 verifier behavior under single-TSA token tamper: check 2 catches the file SHA mismatch; check 6 does NOT (the base bundle has 3 TSAs verified; ONE invalidated still leaves ≥2 verifying, which satisfies spec §11.1).

expected_failures: check 2 (artifact integrity): .tsr file SHA-256 mismatch in manifest.
notes: V1 verifier does NOT fail check 6 on this fixture. Spec §11.1 mandates ≥2-of-3 distinct TSAs verify; the base bundle has 3 TSAs and the tamper invalidates ONE, leaving 2 verifying. A bundle with only 2 TSAs would fail check 6 under this tamper (only 1 verifying); the fixture's structure documents the multi-TSA tolerance.
